pattern: Postorder names the root last; locating that root inside inorder splits the remaining values into the left and right subtrees, recursively.
hints: The last value of postorder is always the current subtree's root.
Find that root inside inorder: values to its left form the left subtree, values to its right the right subtree.
Consume postorder from the back, building the right subtree before the left, with a value-to-inorder-index map for O(1) splits.

edge_inputs.description: balanced tree from the canonical traversals

stress.description: build a random tree, derive consistent inorder+postorder so the answer is unique
